checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2013 Cash Flow Statement (prior year comparative)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet or CostOfGoodsSold/CostOfRevenue concept values were provided in the dataset for SAIA Inc.

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueServicesNet=293022000 (2015-01-01 to 2015-03-31); SalesRevenueServicesNet=323480000 (2015-04-01 to 2015-06-30); SalesRevenueServicesNet=317199000 (2015-07-01 to 2015-09-30)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2016 Balance Sheet and 10-K FY2011 comparative

evidence.quote: No AllowanceForDoubtfulAccounts or specific reserve concept values were provided in the dataset.

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: UPLOAD filed 2012-08-20; CORRESP filed 2012-08-31; UPLOAD filed 2012-09-10
evidence.location: SEC filing chronology - SEC comment letter correspondence 2012

evidence.quote: UPLOAD filed 2016-06-30; CORRESP filed 2016-07-14; UPLOAD filed 2016-08-02
evidence.location: SEC filing chronology - SEC comment letter correspondence 2016

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2003-03-14 through 10-K filed 2017-02-24 filed consistently annually with no NT 10-K/NT 10-Q entries observed in chronology.

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data provided for Assets, AssetsCurrent, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, AccountsPayableCurrent, LiabilitiesCurrent, NetIncomeLoss, OperatingIncomeLoss, SalesRevenueServicesNet, NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities(ContinuingOperations), Depreciation, Goodwill, PropertyPlantAndEquipment(Net/Gross), StockholdersEquity spanning 2008-2016.
No inventory, cost of revenue/COGS, or allowance for doubtful accounts concepts were included, limiting CL4 and part of CL6 assessment.

top_signals: AR/Revenue ratio rose from ~10.1% (FY2014-15) to ~11.1% (FY2016) even as revenue was flat-to-declining (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=135,083,000 (2016-12-31) vs SalesRevenueServicesNet=1,218,481,000 (2016) roughly flat vs 2015), a mild working-capital divergence worth monitoring but not corroborated by other red flags.

Operating cash flow has consistently and substantially exceeded net income every year (e.g., NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=145,776,000 vs NetIncomeLoss=48,024,000 in 2016), which is expected for a capital-intensive LTL trucking carrier with rising depreciation (Depreciation=74,500,000 in 2016) and is generally a reassuring rather than concerning pattern.

Two rounds of SEC comment-letter correspondence (UPLOAD/CORRESP filings in 2012 and again in 2016) indicate routine SEC Division of Corporation Finance review activity; no subsequent restatements, NT filings, or 10-K/A amendments were observed in the chronology, suggesting these were resolved without material financial statement changes.

Balance sheet growth (Assets, PP&E, goodwill from 2015 acquisition, stockholders' equity) tracks reasonably with revenue and operating income growth over 2010-2016, and earnings/revenue seasonality appears consistent quarter-to-quarter, showing no signs of earnings smoothing or channel-stuffing.
